notes: Standard application questions. Add new questions as you encounter them. tier_handling values: boilerplate (no generation), standard (light substitution), personalise (full generation from JD), ask_user (prompt user for angle before generating — Tier 1 only).

questions.label: Salary expectation (free text)
questions.boilerplate: My expectations are in the [market range for role level and geography — load from AGENTS.md Salary Rules]. I'm open to discussing based on the full compensation package and scope of the role.
questions.notes: Anchor at the top of the realistic range for the role level and geography (see AGENTS.md Salary Rules). Never anchor at the floor. Never volunteer the salary floor from profile.search_parameters.

questions.label: Salary expectation (number input only)
questions.boilerplate: Enter the top of the realistic market range for the role level and geography. Load the applicable range from AGENTS.md Salary Rules and use the upper bound of that range.
questions.notes: For senior IC roles: use the top of the geography range from AGENTS.md. Bump upper end further for staff/principal roles.

questions.label: Why do you want to work here?
questions.notes: Must reference something specific to the company — product, mission, a recent decision, a specific team. Never generic ('fast-growing company', 'exciting space'). 3–4 sentences max. For Tier 1: ask the user what angle they want before generating.

questions.label: Note to hiring manager
questions.notes: 2–3 sentences. Not a cover letter, not a CV summary. Lead with the specific fit between the user's background and this role's core problem. Tone: direct, confident, concise. For Tier 1: ask user for their angle first.

questions.label: Anything additional you'd like to share about your application?
questions.notes: Default: leave blank. Only use if there is genuinely something relevant not captured elsewhere — e.g. a portfolio link, a specific project, a timing note. Never pad.
